hypothesis: Introducing aromatic residues at position 5 and hydrophobic residues at position 11 will enhance SSTR2-specific binding by improving complementarity with ECL2/TM5/TM6 regions, while avoiding off-target interactions.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current hypothesis is that the native pharmacophore (FWKT(7-10) and Cys3/Cys14) is sufficient for SSTR2 binding, but off-target binding is driven by conserved interactions at non-pharmacophore positions. We now propose that introducing mutations at positions 1, 2, 5, 6, 11, and 12 with acidic residues will disrupt these off-target interactions and improve selectivity.

proposed_changes.rationale: To improve selectivity, we need to disrupt off-target contacts at non-pharmacophore positions. Focusing mutations on these positions will allow us to fine-tune interactions that are likely contributing to off-target binding (e.g., SSTR3/SSTR5 conserved-pocket contacts).

Introducing acidic residues (D, E) at non-pharmacophore positions may disrupt conserved hydrophobic interactions with off-target SSTRs (e.g., SSTR3/SSTR5) while preserving the native pharmacophore. This will help increase the Δmargin and improve selectivity.
